_meta.vertical: B2B — exotic car rental companies, luxury concierges, hospitality partners
_meta.audience: Owner-operator or sales lead at exotic rental / concierge company — office hours, mobile + main line
_meta.source_note: Aggregated from large B2B outbound datasets. Office-hours pattern, with adjustment for owner-operators who pick up their own cells. Validate per-account with your own dial logs.

core_windows.label: Morning planning window
core_windows.note: Golden hour around 8–9 or 9–10 a.m. — decision-makers are planning the day, not yet in meetings.

core_windows.label: Late afternoon decision window
core_windows.note: 3–5 p.m. consistently strong for reaching B2B decision-makers and qualifying.

experimental_blocks.label: Owner late window
experimental_blocks.note: Smaller exotic rental shops have owner-operators who answer their own cells after staff leaves. Experimental, not core.

experimental_blocks.label: Monday afternoon recovery
experimental_blocks.note: If you have to touch Monday, afternoon is materially better than morning catch-up mode.

avoid_windows.label: Lunch / midday crunch
avoid_windows.reason: B2B contact + qualification dip during the lunch/crunch hours.

avoid_windows.label: Monday morning
avoid_windows.reason: Catch-up mode, less open to new vendors.

avoid_windows.label: After-hours personal time
avoid_windows.reason: Concierge / hospitality prospects guard personal hours. After 5:30–6 p.m. risks family time.

avoid_days.reason: Catch-up mode; weakest for new-vendor outreach.
Wind-down day for office decision-makers; rental ops shift focus to weekend bookings.
Peak rental delivery day — owners are slammed with car prep and client pickups. Avoid.

homeowner_nuances: Exotic rental is a hybrid B2B/B2C business — the owner you're calling is often the lead salesperson AND the car-prep manager. Mid-week midday is rough because they're prepping vehicles for weekend bookings.
Concierge partners answer office lines during business hours. Owner-operators answer cells almost any time but resent off-hours interruptions.
Avoid Friday afternoons entirely — that's when owners are confirming weekend delivery logistics and the last thing they want is a cold pitch.
B2B office-hours pattern dominates. Treat after-hours as experiment, not core block.
